# Tidying {.unnumbered} ![](images/wrangling.png){width="650" fig-align="center"}

**Tidying hones in on just the data you want to analyse.**

The process includes sorting, filtering, and preprocessing raw data so that it can be easily analyzed and visualised. ## Sorting We can sort a dataframe based on the values in one (or more) columns by giving a list to ```.sort_values()```. ::: {.cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.sort_values(['protein', 'donor_id', 'time_h'], inplace=True) df.head(5) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
1 1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 0.203740 2 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
139 139 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 2.120048 4 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
277 277 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm -4.341109 6 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
415 415 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm -2.328065 8 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
553 553 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 9.565341 10 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
``` ::: ::: ## Filtering based on values We can select entries based on their value in a specific column using *comparison operators* like ```>``` , ```<``` and ```==```. ::: {.cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100].head(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
2 2 COL1A1_Collagen 300.0 Structural support Extracellular NaN 2 A17362 COL1A1 Collagen
4 4 MYH7_Myosin 220.0 Muscle contraction Cytoplasm -1.274221 2 A17362 MYH7 Myosin
9 9 FTH1_Ferritin 474.0 Iron storage Cytoplasm 1.321842 2 A17362 FTH1 Ferritin
``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### You can think about this as *"the dataframe where the dataframe in the XX column is equal to YY"*. ::: We can select entries based on their value in a specific column using *comparison operators* like ```>``` , ```<``` and ```==```. ::: {.cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100].head(3) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-important} ### Note that this is creating a *view* of the dataframe. To use the filtered version in other operations, you need to assign a new variable and create a ```copy```. ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} len(df) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` 690 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} len(df[df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ``` 210 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} len(df) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=8} ``` 690 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} new_dataframe = df[df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100].copy() len(new_dataframe) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ``` 210 ``` ::: ::: We can select entries based on their value in a specific column using *comparison operators* like ```>``` , ```<``` and ```==```. ::: {.cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100].head(3) ``` ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=11} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ``` ::: ::: We can chain multiple filters using ```and``` (```&```) notion. ::: {.cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[(df['molecular_weight_kda'] > 100) & (df['subcellular_location'] != 'Cytoplasm')].head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=12} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
2 2 COL1A1_Collagen 300.0 Structural support Extracellular NaN 2 A17362 COL1A1 Collagen
12 12 FGA_Fibrinogen 340.0 Blood clotting Plasma 1.359425 2 A17362 FGA Fibrinogen
``` ::: ::: ## Handling missing values We can filter for rows containing missing values using ```.isna()``` or ```.isnull()```. ::: {.cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[df['expression_log2'].isna()].head(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=13} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
2 2 COL1A1_Collagen 300.0 Structural support Extracellular NaN 2 A17362 COL1A1 Collagen
20 20 HK1_Hexokinase 102.0 Glycolysis Cytoplasm NaN 2 A17362 HK1 Hexokinase
121 121 SOD1_SOD1 32.5 Superoxide breakdown Cytoplasm NaN 2 A39917 SOD1 SOD1
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=14} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ``` ::: ::: Missing values can be removed using ```.dropna()```. ::: {.cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.dropna(axis=0, how='all', subset=['expression_log2']).head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=15} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
0 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm 0.111781 2 A17362 HBB Hemoglobin
1 1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 0.203740 2 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
``` ::: ::: Alternatively, you may wish to fill missing values specifically using ```.fillna()``` ::: {.cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.fillna(0, inplace=True) # Works on any datatype df.head(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=16} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
0 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm 0.111781 2 A17362 HBB Hemoglobin
1 1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 0.203740 2 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
2 2 COL1A1_Collagen 300.0 Structural support Extracellular 0.000000 2 A17362 COL1A1 Collagen
``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### Pandas also provides some limited interpolation using ```.interpolate()```, however purpose-built libraries provide more sophisticated options. ::: ## Basic arithmetic We can do arithmetic on whole columns simultaneously, like calculating the ```mean``` or ```sum``` of numerical values using inbuilt functions or mathematical operators. ::: {.cell execution_count=17} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.count() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=18} ``` Unnamed: 0 690 protein 690 molecular_weight_kda 690 function 690 subcellular_location 690 expression_log2 690 time_h 690 donor_id 690 protein_name 690 gene 690 dtype: int64 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=19} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.mean() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` C:\Users\Dezerae\AppData\Local\Temp\ipykernel_13132\3698961737.py:1: FutureWarning: Dropping of nuisance columns in DataFrame reductions (with 'numeric_only=None') is deprecated; in a future version this will raise TypeError. Select only valid columns before calling the reduction. df.mean() ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=20} ``` Unnamed: 0 344.500000 molecular_weight_kda 113.104348 expression_log2 -0.021831 time_h 6.000000 dtype: float64 ``` ::: ::: We can do arithmetic on whole columns simultaneously, like calculating the ```mean``` or ```sum``` of numerical values using inbuilt functions or mathematical operators. ::::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="45%"} ::: {.cell execution_count=21} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['molecular_weight_kda'] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=21} ``` 0 64.5 1 42.0 2 300.0 3 5.8 4 220.0 ... 685 184.0 686 110.0 687 210.0 688 40.0 689 55.0 Name: molecular_weight_kda, Length: 690, dtype: float64 ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column width="55%"} ::: {.cell execution_count=22} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['molecular_weight_kda'] * 1000 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=22} ``` 0 64500.0 1 42000.0 2 300000.0 3 5800.0 4 220000.0 ... 685 184000.0 686 110000.0 687 210000.0 688 40000.0 689 55000.0 Name: molecular_weight_kda, Length: 690, dtype: float64 ``` ::: ::: ::: ::::: We can do arithmetic on whole columns simultaneously, like calculating the ```mean``` or ```sum``` of numerical values using inbuilt functions or mathematical operators. ::: {.cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['mw_daltons'] = df['molecular_weight_kda'] * 1000 df.head(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=23} ```{=html}
Unnamed: 0 protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene mw_daltons
0 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm 0.111781 2 A17362 HBB Hemoglobin 64500.0
1 1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 0.203740 2 A17362 ACTA1 Actin 42000.0
2 2 COL1A1_Collagen 300.0 Structural support Extracellular 0.000000 2 A17362 COL1A1 Collagen 300000.0
``` ::: ::: ## Operating on groups What if we want to perform arithmetic not on the whole dataframe, but on subsets? We can operate on chunks of a dataframe simultaneously using ```groupby```. ![](images/groupby.png){width="600" fig-align="center"} ::: {.cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} loc_counts = df.groupby(['subcellular_location']).count()[['protein']] loc_counts ``` ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=25} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=25} ```{=html}
protein
subcellular_location
Cytoplasm 315
Extracellular 135
Membrane 15
``` ::: ::: What if we want to perform arithmetic not on the whole dataframe, but on subsets? We can operate on chunks of a dataframe simultaneously using ```groupby```. ![](images/groupby.png){width="600" fig-align="center"} ::: {.callout-note} ### Think of this like creating filtered copies of the dataframe, conducting the operation, then combining them all back together again! ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### ```groupby``` is an iterator object which is not created until it is evaluated. Operations used with groupby create views and are *not* operating on the original dataframe. ::: We can access each *chunk* of the dataframe using a ```for``` loop: ::: {.cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code} for loc, data in df.groupby(['subcellular_location']): print( f'The average molecular weight of proteins in the {loc} is \ {int(data["molecular_weight_kda"].mean())} kDa.') ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` The average molecular weight of proteins in the Cytoplasm is 76 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Extracellular is 277 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Membrane is 88 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Mitochondria is 35 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Nucleus is 37 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Peroxisome is 60 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Plasma is 340 kDa. The average molecular weight of proteins in the Secreted is 53 kDa. ``` ::: ::: ## In practice ::: {.callout-caution} ### Exercises 3.2 Filtering and arithmetic 3.3 Grouped operations :::
